title: A local description of strong symmetries and strong-to-weak symmetry breaking in quantum many-body systems
authors: Ruizhi Liu, Jinmin Yi, Dominic V. Else
abstract: In mixed states of quantum systems, symmetries come in two types: strong and weak. Furthermore, it has been argued that in quantum many-body systems, strong symmetries can be "spontaneously broken" down to weak symmetries. An issue is that as previously formulated, such "strong-to-weak symmetry breaking" appears to be a fairly non-local effect. In this paper, we show how to understand and diagnose strong symmetries and strong-to-weak symmetry breaking in an explicitly local way. Our main technical tool is a rigorous definition of strong symmetry in the limit of infinite volume, which generalizes the conventional finite-volume definitions, and for which we give several equivalent formulations, including one involving the concept of "local charge coherence". Finally, we introduce von Neumann systems, which in infinite-volume symmetries are intermediate between strong and weak symmetries. We derive a Lieb-Schultz-Mattis type anomaly constraint for von Neumann symmetries (and therefore, in particular, strong symmetries) in quantum spin chains.
summary: This paper develops a rigorous, local mathematical framework to redefine and diagnose strong symmetries and the process of strong-to-weak symmetry breaking in quantum many-body systems. By utilizing operator algebras in the infinite volume limit, the authors establish that strong symmetry is equivalent to a state being 'charge-coherent.' This provides a powerful, local tool for understanding symmetry constraints in complex quantum matter.
main_problem: The paper aims to provide a mathematically rigorous and local description for strong symmetries and the process of strong-to-weak symmetry breaking (SW-SSB) in quantum many-body systems.
main_result: The authors establish that strong symmetry is equivalent to the state being 'charge-coherent' in the infinite volume limit, providing a local diagnostic tool for previously non-local phenomena.
method: The methodology relies heavily on the algebraic approach using local operator algebras ($C^*$-algebras) to define states and symmetries in the thermodynamic limit, avoiding the difficulties of infinite tensor products.
model_or_system: The focus is on general quantum many-body systems, particularly in mixed (non-pure) states, analyzed on an infinite lattice ($\Lambda$). The framework is general enough to apply to quantum spin chains.
key_observables: Strong symmetry, weak symmetry, charge coherence, fidelity, and the anomaly index (an element in $H^3(G, U(1))$).
important_parameters: The infinite volume limit (thermodynamic limit), the nature of the symmetry group $G$ (compact), and the locality radius $r_\alpha$ of the symmetry action.
main_assumptions: The primary assumption is that distinguishing phases of matter should be possible via the expectation values of local operators in the thermodynamic limit. The algebraic approach is necessary because the Hilbert space formalism fails in infinite systems.
paper_structure: The paper progresses by first establishing the limitations of previous non-local formulations, then introducing the rigorous algebraic framework for local observables, defining strong symmetry via charge coherence, and finally applying this to derive constraints like LSM-type anomalies.
why_interesting: This work provides essential mathematical machinery for characterizing phases of matter in open or mixed quantum systems, directly addressing the conceptual gap between symmetry breaking diagnostics and local observables.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. The graphical construction of a bath evolution that sends a state with strong symmetry to one with only weak symmetry, see the text.
